decisions.title: Prioritize Parquet performance optimization vs new Python Rules features
decisions.context: Multiple Parquet tickets (ENG-5724, ENG-6364, ENG-6460) show ongoing investment in data ingestion performance. Need to decide if this accelerates 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' more than expanding Python Rules capabilities.

arguments.for: Faster data ingestion directly enables more decisions per customer by reducing time-to-insight. Parquet optimization is foundational infrastructure.
arguments.against: Python Rules expansion is more visible to customers and directly addresses 'Embed Deeper' pillar. Parquet work is invisible to end users.

decisions.title: Resolve policy creation UX limitations blocking Canvas adoption
decisions.context: ENG-8377 (Set intersection in Policy UI), ENG-9098 (user group attribute conditions), and ENG-9106 (Attributes assign UX) indicate policy creation experience gaps that may limit Canvas effectiveness.

arguments.for: Canvas is a 2026 flagship product. Policy creation friction directly reduces decisions captured. These are table-stakes features.
arguments.against: Core stability issues (GetPhaseAnnotations latency ENG-8874, Saved Panel errors ENG-9109) may be more urgent. Feature expansion can wait.

decisions.title: Address GetPhaseAnnotations API latency before Q2 customer expansion
decisions.context: ENG-8874 shows API performance issues. With 'Nail the Core' as pillar #1, need to decide if performance work blocks new customer onboarding.
decisions.blockedBy: Root cause analysis incomplete, no SLO baseline established

arguments.for: Performance degradation will compound with customer growth. Fix now before scaling. Aligns with 'Nail the Core'.
arguments.against: May be isolated edge case. Customer acquisition velocity shouldn't wait for performance optimization that affects <5% of queries.

decisions.title: Commit to K2 Mission data pipeline or defer custom integrations
decisions.context: ENG-9104 represents custom pipeline work for specific customer. Decision needed: is this 'Embed Deeper' validation or distraction from platform work?

arguments.for: Deep customer embedding validates product-market fit and generates reference architecture. Could unlock aerospace vertical.
arguments.against: Custom pipelines don't scale. Resources better spent on self-service data connectors (Sift Edge expansion) that serve all customers.

decisions.title: Finalize AWS savings plan commitment vs spot instance strategy
decisions.context: ENG-9068 evaluating AWS savings plans. Need to decide infrastructure cost strategy as data volumes grow.

arguments.for: Savings plans lock in 30-40% cost reduction, improving unit economics. Predictable costs enable aggressive customer acquisition.
arguments.against: Commitment reduces flexibility as architecture evolves. Spot instances + autoscaling may be more cost-effective for variable workloads.

keyResults.name: Zero critical UX errors in saved configs

timeAudit.weekSummary: 38% of week in meetings. Engineering weighted toward reactive bug fixes (9 stability tickets) vs proactive feature work (7 tickets). Policy UX and Parquet work show coordinated efforts.
alerts: Only 2 focus blocks today - insufficient for deep technical decisions on API latency and AWS savings plans
3 one-on-ones today may indicate brewing people/process issues - follow up on team velocity concerns
20% of Linear tickets are infrastructure/dev tooling (EC2, vector, local data mode) - check if platform tech debt is accumulating
